description: Cybersecurity interview questions for junior or entry-level candidates.

questions.question: An employee receives an email asking them to click a suspicious link and enter their password. What should they do first?

choices.text: Click the link quickly to check if it is real.
Report the email through the proper security channel and avoid clicking the link.
Forward it to all coworkers without warning.
Reply with their password to confirm their identity.

questions.correctExplanation: Suspicious emails should be reported and not interacted with. This reduces the risk of phishing, credential theft, or malware.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Clicking suspicious links can expose credentials or install malware.
Forwarding the email widely may spread the risk.
Passwords should never be shared by email.

questions.question: Why is multi-factor authentication useful?

choices.text: It adds an extra layer of protection beyond just a password.
It makes passwords unnecessary in every situation.
It guarantees that accounts can never be compromised.
It is only used to make login pages look more complex.

questions.correctExplanation: Multi-factor authentication reduces risk by requiring an additional proof of identity, such as a code, device, or biometric factor.

wrongExplanations.explanation: MFA often works together with passwords rather than replacing them completely.
MFA greatly reduces risk but cannot guarantee perfect security.
The purpose of MFA is security, not visual complexity.

questions.question: What does the principle of least privilege mean?

choices.text: Users should receive only the access they need to perform their job.
Every employee should have administrator access.
Access controls should be removed to improve speed.
Passwords should be shared among team members.

questions.correctExplanation: Least privilege limits access to what is necessary, reducing damage if an account is misused or compromised.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Giving everyone administrator access increases risk.
Removing access controls may create serious security problems.
Password sharing reduces accountability and increases risk.

questions.question: A junior analyst notices unusual login activity on a user account. What is the best first response?

choices.text: Ignore it unless the user complains.
Document the observation, follow the incident response process, and escalate to the appropriate team.
Delete all logs to protect privacy.
Immediately announce the incident publicly.

questions.correctExplanation: Security events should be documented and handled through the proper incident response process.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Ignoring unusual activity can allow an incident to grow.
Logs are important evidence and should not be deleted.
Public communication should follow approved incident procedures.

questions.question: Why is patch management important in cybersecurity?

choices.text: It helps fix known vulnerabilities before attackers can exploit them.
It guarantees that no future vulnerabilities will exist.
It is only useful for changing the visual design of software.
It should always be skipped to avoid downtime.

questions.correctExplanation: Patch management reduces exposure to known security weaknesses, especially when vulnerabilities are actively exploited.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Patching reduces known risk but cannot prevent all future vulnerabilities.
Patches often address security, stability, or functionality, not just visuals.
Downtime should be managed, but skipping patches can leave systems vulnerable.
